content.text:
{
  "dimension": "coaching-stance",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach holds VIEW throughout. Pace: when the client rushes into list-recitation in turns 3, 5, 7, 9, the coach consistently slows down rather than matching — turns 4, 8, 10, 14 are short, single questions with breathing room. Impartiality: the coach refuses the client's repeated invitations to strategize ('can we just map out the week?', 'what do I actually need to have ready?') without ever moralizing about the deflection — they simply don't take the bait and redirect to what's alive. Wonder: questions land on the specific phenomenon in front of them (the stopped sentence, the dismissal, the heaviness) rather than generic 'what's underneath' probing. Empathy: turn 12 ('Don't apologize. That came from somewhere true') meets the client's nervous laugh with steadiness rather than reassurance-noise. There is no preacher-mode, no 'you create your overwhelm' reflection, no breath-as-repression. The coach holds silence implicitly (turn 14's 'Yeah.' before the question) and lets the client's pauses land. Slight ding: no first-person vulnerability from the coach ('I notice I want to...') — but this is not required for a 3 when the other components are this consistent.",
  "evidence_turns": [4, 8, 10, 12, 14],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Opens with a feeling question — fine but slightly stacked (offers three options); not yet showing pace mismatch."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the move from feeling to list without judgment; single question, slower pace than client."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Short, open question. Doesn't take the strategize-bait from turn 5."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Tracks the somatic/verbal interruption rather than the content of 'map out the week.' Genuine noticing, not script."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Refuses to let the imposter-syndrome dismissal stand; invites the thing to 'just sit there' — classic VIEW move from openness, not agenda."},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Meets the client's apologetic deflection with warmth and steadiness; pace is slow, presence is felt."},
    {"turn": 14, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "The 'Yeah.' before the question is real meeting. Question opens the false-end territory without forcing it."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["the-rush-signature", "meet-resistance-with-okay", "what-are-you-defending"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["calibrate-the-threshold"],
  "notes": "Coach never offers own state/vulnerability explicitly, but the impartiality and wonder are strong enough that this still reads as full VIEW rather than 2."
}
